content:
5 min read
Just now

--

After 18+ years in software development, I’ve noticed a pattern. The developers who become architects aren’t necessarily the smartest coders. They’re the ones who learn to solve a different kind of problem.
The Uncomfortable Truth
Most developers believe becoming an architect is a natural next step.
Work hard.
Learn more C#.
Master .NET.
Collect certifications.
Get promoted.
Become an architect.
Sounds logical.
Except that’s not how it works.
I’ve met developers with 20 years of experience who are still solving the same problems they solved 10 years ago.
I’ve also met architects who reached that role in half the time.
The difference wasn’t intelligence.
The difference wasn’t coding ability.
And surprisingly, it wasn’t technical knowledge.
The difference was how they thought.
The Career Trap Almost Every Developer Falls Into
Most developers spend their careers asking implementation questions.
Questions like:

• Which design pattern should I use?

• Should I choose EF…
